name: Turbulence
domain: Chaos, sensitive dependence, fractal cascades, strange attractors
core_insight: Chaotic systems are deterministic yet unpredictable beyond their Lyapunov horizon. Energy cascades from large eddies to small following a universal -5/3 power law. Bifurcation sequences have universal constants (Feigenbaum) — chaos is structured at a deep level.

key_equations.name: Lorenz Attractor
key_equations.formula: x' = σ(y - x); y' = x(ρ - z) - y; z' = xy - βz
key_equations.why: Archetypical strange attractor: bounded, non-periodic, sensitive to initial conditions

key_equations.name: Kolmogorov Turbulence Scaling
key_equations.why: Energy spectrum of fully developed turbulence: universal regardless of fluid or forcing scale

key_equations.name: Feigenbaum Constants
key_equations.why: Universal ratios governing period-doubling route to chaos — same for any smooth 1D map

seed_prompt: ᘯᘰ Compute the Lyapunov exponent. Positive means chaos. The attractor has structure — find its fractal dimension.
